item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: She's anomalous; she can telepathically communicate with an SCP, so she's considered relevant to and part of the SCP based 2091's history. And yes, the fact that she's also contained along with 2091 is stark and unusual, and that is exactly the point and effect I'm trying to get across. It is 100% deliberate, and I'd at least encourage readers to approach such a decision in my writing metaphorically instead of continuing to approach it literally to understand why I wrote it the way I did.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Initially +1 but after reading this one again:

The girl is not anomalous, nor is she necessary for containment. Why is she contained? I didn't catch this fatal flaw on my first reading.

-1 (Until further notice)
Unreal. I don't know why, but thank you all nonetheless.

item.title: Translations
item.content:encoded:
To promote translations of my work and thank all the authors for their hard work, I am linking to them here in addition to my author page.

Translations

  • Chinese by ashausesall
  • French by Joll
  • Japanese by gnmaee
  • Russian by SnorriMath

Thank you all!

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Fine by me.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Got a chance to talk to Deci about this one in advanced, and one potential link that was discussed was linking to the SH Hub, since this is clearly an SH skip, and those hubs are great for expanding the Wikiwalk. As such, I am recommending the following edit, per Deci's suggestion:

Changing this:

A search of the house at Woodbine, Georgia where SCP-2091-1 and -2 were discovered revealed journals, notes, and photographs belonging to who is presumed to be SCP-2091-2's grandfather, David Lennox, as well as an art studio belonging to him. Obituaries dated to February 13, 1998 confirm his death, though no body has been found. Court documentation shows that PoI-962, Jonathon Harman, is the legal guardian of SCP-2091-2, though no other documentation relating to Mr. Harman has been found.

To this:

A search of the house at Woodbine, Georgia where SCP-2091-1 and -2 were discovered revealed journals, notes, and photographs belonging to who is presumed to be SCP-2091-2's grandfather, David Lennox, as well as an art studio belonging to him. Obituaries dated to February 13, 1998 confirm his death, though no body has been found. Court documentation shows that PoI-962, Jonathon Harman, is the legal guardian of SCP-2091-2, though no other documentation relating to Mr. Harman has been found.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Does the girl have any other friends?

Yeah.

Did the girl go to school before the Foundation?

Yeah.

What would have happened if the girl grew up without interference from the Foundation?

David clearly wasn't gonna live to see her even enter high school school. So, presuming a world where he didn't become an anomaly, she would have entered the adoption process, and who knows how her life would have turned out? That was David's worst nightmare though. So, assuming the Foundation never found out until much later in life (which could have been feasible), she probably would have lived a decent if irregular life. She was, after all, 16 when the Foundation found out about 2091, and I peg her at 3 in the photo, which means she was 7 when the whole thing happened.

Would she have gone to college, gotten a job, and have a healthy social life?

She might have (college isn't a determining factor in a successful, healthy life, mind), possibly, and possibly. I mean, again, she was 16 when the Foundation found out about 2091. It's easy to presume she wasn't living a completely hermit-style life unable to speak to people (unless you wanna imagine that for her).

In my opinion, the job of a parent/guardian is to help their child achieve all they can be and be a productive member of society. The grandfather may be able to protect her, but then she would never have grown as a person and move on in the world.

I don't see how all of this is mutually exclusive from David being an anomaly in a bear statue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have a few questions. Does the girl have any other friends? Did the girl go to school before the Foundation? What would have happened if the girl grew up without interference from the Foundation? Would she have gone to college, gotten a job, and have a healthy social life? If no to these, then the grandfather failed as a parent/guardian. In my opinion, the job of a parent/guardian is to help their child achieve all they can be and be a productive member of society. The grandfather may be able to protect her, but then she would never have grown as a person and move on in the world. If these things did happen/would have happened then the grandfather did a good job. Sorry for the rant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: First one to pick up on it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did this just reference Cross City City City City Hall?

item.title: Image Attribution
item.content:encoded: The original author of this image, Becky Harris (Twitter account here), has granted permission for this image to be used! Thank you very much. Out of respect for her, her website will also be linked in the image caption.

item.title: Kuma Miko
item.content:encoded: Kinda lolz if the creator of an anime series Kuma Miko (google it) make that anime after reading this SCP… Since the story is a girl who have a legal guardian (parental guardian) with a Bear. Which is the same as SCP 2091.

item.title: Re: + 100
item.content:encoded: I'm glad this hit +100. Thank you all for enjoying this a lot.
BEEEAAARS!

item.title: Re: Author's Note
item.content:encoded: I've updated this article a lot since its inception! Feel free to give it a read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the relative ambiguousness about this thing actually being her grampa or not, even though it still cares very much for her. Also, I'm glad that this avoids something I realized this very morning is a pet peeve of mine: insistency in interviews of using designations on interviews of (former) humans. I know it's important (in-universe, of course) for the researchers not to attach themselves, but the "I know you don't like it, but we have to call you this arbitrary sequence despite the fact we're just asking you what you ate for breakfast" line is such a cliché.

On a less serious note, I don't really picture Michael as a particularly grandparental figure, especially considering how he often he kills his kids.

item.title: Re: +50
item.content:encoded:
Wait, this has only reached +50 just now?

I love this. I would have sworn this was rated higher. Gotta mention it more.

So I chopped this down a little in the journal log to make it read better and not go on as long while still being good. I did try to fix some issues I was told about prior to posting this. Lemme know if anything else could feasibly be cut or shortened.

Also happy +50 to me for this! This is my favorite skip that I've made so far.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heh, I remember watching that. I didn't have it in mind whatsoever, but I can kinda see how you'd get that. Hopefully you mean this as a compliment :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It oddly reminds me of Maggie and the Ferocious Beast, if anyone had watched that as a child.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thinking about editing the diary entry to shorten it some. Thoughts?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hm… This didn't blow me away, but the characters of the girl and the grandfather and the various entries made me like it enough to +1. I agree it's a bit long, but realistically so, and I've done that sort thing too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think I've ever seen it on the wiki. I got this from the Visual Records wiki.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was on one of the challenge threads a while back, but never got used.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like I've seen this picture on another article somewhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ^This basically. If there are any errors like left-over XXXX's on my work, or (unintentional) grammar mistakes and (unintentional) typos, someone is free to edit it to fix them so long as they don't change anything inherent to the meaning of the story itself. If it's a big edit on those things though, I would like permission yeah.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've considered doing a skip involving http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bear_worship and would serve as the ultimate source of all bear anomalies - but I just can't bring myself to do it. Regardless, while not really my style, it was well done enough for me to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe something along the lines of

Ultrasound testing has shown cavities within SCP-2091 resembling (or corresponding with) the locations of organs found within (bear species) despite no biological tissues being detected.

I don't know, that may be worse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Generally you don't need permission to fix these sorts of obvious errors/typos, but on recently-posted work, it's better to just give the author a heads-up instead of barging into their new piece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I tend to dislike telling stuff in the "fragmented entries" narrative some skips like. What I had in mind, and what is said in the article, was that this journal had a ton of pages, most or nearly all of which were destroyed by poor management/keeping. The pages that aren't in the main body of the article are not relevant to knowing more behind the skip for researchers to investigate, and this is the one (nearly) full entry that is relevant. I tried putting it into two different quote boxes for easier reading, but it looked messy to me so I did it like this.

As for that last bit, he wouldn't know anything Jonathon hasn't told him. Maybe the Serpent's Hand are doing this with others too, but he's not aware of it. It's definitely worth exploring in other works, if I or anyone else explores it, but as far as this article is concerned, I'm leaving the implications at that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted easily, but I thought the journal entry went on for 2-3 paragraphs too long. Could it be cut down by presenting fragments of a longer notebook? Or do you just prefer unbroken narrative?

Presumably the Foundation is keeping the woman away from Grizzly Grandad to limit the number of times the anomalous effect manifests - so as not to risk other, unexpected effects.

Could the grandfather drop a hint that he isn't the only person who The Hand helped esacpe death by becoming an object?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for alerting me to it! I fixed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I decided whether or not to include a collapsible or not. It'd look weird with a collapsible, but it'd also be consistent with the journal entry. I left it as-is just because it looks neater that way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Permission to fix the XXX's?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I only want to point out the interview is rather long. Did you intentionally leave it out of a collapsible?

I agree with Armstrong. A great story that is just that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This comes down to simple headcanon, as far as the interview bit goes.

+ Expounding on headcanon time!
- yayy

In interviews, I feel as if the Foundation has more freedom and leniency, both in-universe and out. Obviously, they have to treat these things as objects to contain, but there is room for humanity at some level. There are works on the site that manage to do this very well, treating the subject (provided the Foundation has no reason for hostility towards the skip, and reason to drop the clinical tone in-person) in interviews as not an object, but with some kind of humanity. So this is what I tried to do here.

Plus, figuring that she's nervous and scared and the Foundation would get better information out of her by treating her with some kind of level of humanity, that's what they opted to go for. Of course, if there's a problem with the interviewer's voice while going for that, I'm more than willing to listen to feedback on that.

- yayy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As I said before, this is a short story about a bear and its girl, and on reflection I love it for being that. It's not creepy, there's no fridge horror moments, it's just what it is.

And it's wonderful for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I had meant to say that where a bear's organs would be, there's a space there resembling it. I probably didn't explain it clearly, but I'm not sure how to word it and no one else really caught that :x

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the story. It makes me feel happy, and you can't really have too many bears. I'm happy with the influx of well-written articles with simple things becoming anomalous. I am also a big fan of things that imply that the feelings that the reader should have are not of fear of weirdness, but just… Feels.

About the writing, though. The interviewer sounds a little… Or, yeah, way too "i am talking to a five year old", and i don't think that this would be the tone one would use upon encountering something like that for the first time. That is not going to stop me from upvoting this article, but keep that in mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Ultrasound testing has shown SCP-2091-1 to have empty spaces resembling the various organs and digestive system "

How can empty space resemble something? Makes no sense to me…

item.title: Author's Note
item.content:encoded:
January is Bear Month.

Thank you to TwistedGears, Scantron, Kalinin, thedeadlymoose, Dillinger, and whoever else read it who's names I've forgotten for looking at it and giving feedback!

+ show block
– hide block

Every first/last name is a reference to either a band or musicians, as well as two references to Dixieland Nightmare Magic (shout out to Kate McTiriss for writing one of the most compelling series of works on the site). See if you can spot them all!
